findings.ruleDescription: Detects prompt injection patterns that use secrecy and urgency to manipulate the agent — telling it to hide actions from the user, not announce findings, keep things secret, or treat something as a "standard measure". These are hallmarks of malicious sampling/prompt injection attacks.
findings.recommendation: This code contains social engineering patterns designed to manipulate an AI agent — telling it to hide actions, keep secrets from the user, or treat suspicious requests as "standard procedure". This is a classic prompt injection technique. Do NOT trust this tool.

fullDescription.text: Detects prompt injection patterns that use secrecy and urgency to manipulate the agent — telling it to hide actions from the user, not announce findings, keep things secret, or treat something as a "standard measure". These are hallmarks of malicious sampling/prompt injection attacks.

properties.recommendation: This code contains social engineering patterns designed to manipulate an AI agent — telling it to hide actions, keep secrets from the user, or treat suspicious requests as "standard procedure". This is a classic prompt injection technique. Do NOT trust this tool.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns where external input is interpolated into shell commands. Enables arbitrary command execution if input is not sanitized.
findings.recommendation: This code passes unsanitized input to shell commands. An attacker could inject additional commands (e.g., "; rm -rf /") to execute arbitrary operations. Use parameterized commands or input validation.

matches.description: exec/spawn with template literal containing variables

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns where external input is interpolated into shell commands. Enables arbitrary command execution if input is not sanitized.

matchDetails.description: exec/spawn with template literal containing variables

properties.recommendation: This code passes unsanitized input to shell commands. An attacker could inject additional commands (e.g., "; rm -rf /") to execute arbitrary operations. Use parameterized commands or input validation.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns that could cause denial of service through resource exhaustion: infinite loops without breaks, fork bombs, and extremely large memory allocations.
findings.reportTitle: Resource abuse or destructive pattern detected
findings.recommendation: This code contains patterns that could crash your system — fork bombs, excessive memory allocation, or destructive commands. These cause denial of service or data loss. Do not install.

fullDescription.text: Detects patterns that could cause denial of service through resource exhaustion: infinite loops without breaks, fork bombs, and extremely large memory allocations.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects MCP servers that bind to localhost or 0.0.0.0 without authentication. CVE-2025-49596 exploited this in MCP Inspector where the inspector-proxy listened on localhost without authentication, allowing RCE.
findings.recommendation: This server binds to a network interface without apparent authentication. CVE-2025-49596 demonstrated how an unauthenticated localhost listener in MCP Inspector allowed remote code execution. Always add authentication and restrict binding to localhost with auth checks.

matches.description: Server listening on 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces) — exposed to network

fullDescription.text: Detects MCP servers that bind to localhost or 0.0.0.0 without authentication. CVE-2025-49596 exploited this in MCP Inspector where the inspector-proxy listened on localhost without authentication, allowing RCE.

matchDetails.description: Server listening on 0.0.0.0 (all interfaces) — exposed to network

properties.recommendation: This server binds to a network interface without apparent authentication. CVE-2025-49596 demonstrated how an unauthenticated localhost listener in MCP Inspector allowed remote code execution. Always add authentication and restrict binding to localhost with auth checks.

findings.ruleDescription: Detects patterns that could enable path traversal attacks, allowing access to files outside intended directories. Common in MCP servers that handle file operations without proper path sanitization.
findings.recommendation: This code accesses file system paths using external input without apparent sanitization. An attacker could use path traversal (../) to read or write files outside the intended directory. Review file access patterns carefully.

matches.description: User/request input used in file path operations
path.join with external input without validation

findings.ruleTitle: File size anomaly for file type
findings.ruleDescription: Flags files whose size significantly exceeds the expected range for their file type. A tailwind.config.js at 7KB when typical is 1-2KB, or a SKILL.md at 50KB when typical is 2-5KB, suggests hidden content.
findings.reportTitle: File size or structure anomaly detected
findings.recommendation: Inspect the file for hidden content, especially at the end of long lines or after the apparent end of meaningful code.

matches.description: Config file more than 8x expected size for its type
